sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `consambig` derives an ambiguity-aware consensus sequence from a single aligned FASTA or Stockholm alignment. The current EMBOSS-RS implementation ignores gaps when tallying a column, emits IUPAC nucleotide ambiguity symbols when multiple exact nucleotide bases remain, and uses `X` for ambiguous protein columns.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface accepts exactly one local aligned FASTA or Stockholm alignment path.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The result is one ambiguity-aware consensus sequence record emitted through the shared sequence result path. CLI output follows the standard sequence-output rendering path.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs consambig`. Current Rust service coverage exercises the committed three-row Stockholm fixture and locks down the ambiguity-aware consensus sequence `ACYGT`.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release only covers the current built-in ambiguity policy. It does not expose alternative consensus alphabets, configurable thresholds, or richer legacy reporting fields.

artifacts.label: Multiple-alignment Stockholm fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed Stockholm alignment fixture used to validate ambiguity-aware consensus derivation.

examples.title: Derive an ambiguity-aware consensus from a three-row Stockholm alignment
examples.description: Loads the committed multiple-alignment Stockholm fixture and reports the ambiguity-aware consensus sequence under the current IUPAC-aware policy.

expected_outputs.label: Ambiguity-aware consensus sequence
expected_outputs.description: A consensus sequence record whose residues are `ACYGT` for the committed fixture.
